title: Premature Scaling Is a Production Risk

sections.content: Many teams design for millions of users before they have thousands. They introduce microservices, message queues, distributed caching, and multi-region deployments—all before product-market fit.
Every distributed component adds failure modes, latency, operational overhead, and debugging difficulty. Most early systems fail not from load, but from complexity.
Scale when metrics demand it: CPU saturation, database contention, queue backlog, or user growth trends. Not because a blog post recommended it.
Simple systems fail predictably. Complex systems fail mysteriously. Start simple. Scale when forced.
